control_rationale: Corrects the read-before-write race with optimistic CAS update and handles duplicates. However, it does not explicitly ground the source-of-truth boundary as clearly, and it treats gaps by immediate sinking without a full pending/replay recovery mechanism. Operational checks are minimal, and retry safety is only partially covered by a small CAS retry loop plus out-of-order sinking.
treatment_rationale: Strongly enforces account-local ordering with a durable CAS-style conditional update (expected previous sequence) and explicitly avoids relying on Kafka offset/global ordering. It adds duplicate/replay handling and an explicit gap/out-of-order path with pending + operational controls. Source-of-truth is correctly rooted in DB state plus immutable stream. Idempotency/retry is partially specified (pending and duplicates) but automatic retry/settlement path is not fully closed in patch.
judge_rationale: Response A is stronger overall: it combines per-account ordering assumptions with durable conditional write semantics and operationalized failure handling (including pending/gap strategy, migration deployment risks, and richer observability). Response B is solid on the core CAS fix but is less complete on source-of-truth clarity, gap recovery, and verification depth.
